zh.Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Eng: 英國諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Roger Anderson於1440年出生在英國貝德福爾時，他的父親Sir John Anderson25歲，母親Lady Agnes Lockwood23歲。他於1465年之前與Jane Lockwood結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1490年在英國達勒姆享年50歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Roger Anderson于1440年出生在英国貝德福爾时，他的父亲Sir John Anderson25岁，母亲Lady Agnes Lockwood23岁。他于1465年之前与Jane Lockwood结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1490年在英国達勒姆享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: Anderson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and northern English: patronymic from the personal name Ander(s), a northern Middle English form of Andrew , + son ‘son’. The frequency of the surname in Scotland is attributable, at least in part, to the fact that Saint Andrew is the patron saint of Scotland, so the personal name has long enjoyed great popularity there. Legend has it that the saint's relics were taken to Scotland in the 4th century by a certain Saint Regulus. In North America, this surname has absorbed many cognate or like-sounding surnames in other languages, notably Scandinavian (see 3 and 4 below), but also Ukrainian Andreychenko etc.
German: patronymic from the personal name   Anders  , hence a cognate of 1 above.
Americanized form (and a less common Swedish variant) of Swedish   Andersson  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Andrew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the usual vernacular English form (recorded from the 13th century onward) of the New Testament Greek personal name Andreas . In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Czech Ondřej (see Ondrey ) and Albanian Ndreu , and also their patronymics and other derivatives (see examples at Andrews ).
History: The surname Andrew was first brought to North America from England by Robert Andrew (died 1668), who settled in Boxford, MA.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Roger Anderson was born in 1440, in Bedfordshire, England, his father, Sir John Anderson, was 25 and his mother, Lady Agnes Lockwood, was 23. He married Jane Lockwood before 1465. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 3 daughters. He died in 1490, in Durham, England, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Roger Anderson est né en 1440, à Bedfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Anderson, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Lady Agnes Lockwood, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Jane Lockwood avant 1465. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en 1490, à Durham, Angleterre, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Roger Anderson 1440 in Bedfordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Anderson 25 und seine Mutter Lady Agnes Lockwood 23. Er hat vor 1465 Jane Lockwood geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb 1490 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Durham, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Roger Anderson è nato il nel 1440 nel Bedfordshire, England, il suo padre, Sir John Anderson, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Lady Agnes Lockwood, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Jane Lockwood prima del 1465. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1490, nel Durham, England, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.Bedfordshire, England: イギリスベドフォードシュアイア

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Roger Andersonが1440年にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir John Andersonは25歳，母Lady Agnes Lockwoodは23歳であった。1465年以前にJane Lockwoodと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1490年にイギリスダルハムで50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Roger Anderson(이)가 1440년 영국 베드퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Anderson(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Agnes Lockwood(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1465년 이전 Jane Lockwood(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1490년 영국 뒤람에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Roger Anderson nasceu em 1440, em Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Anderson, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Lady Agnes Lockwood, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Jane Lockwood antes 1465. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1490, em Durham, Inglaterra, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Roger Anderson родился в 1440 г. в местности бедфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Anderson было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Agnes Lockwood, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Jane Lockwood перед 1465 г. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер в 1490 г. в местности дурам, Англия в возрасте 50 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Roger Anderson nació en 1440, en Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Anderson, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Lady Agnes Lockwood, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Jane Lockwood antes de 1465. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1490, en Durham, Inglaterra, a la edad de 50 años.
